Title: INVENTORY-01 Split inventory into true local food bank inventory in phase two
Body: Background: Inventory still behaves like a platform-level operations view. Goal: Introduce a bank-level inventory model in phase two. Acceptance: Define the data model API scope and migration plan.

Title: CASH-01 Add allocation audit trail only if directed cash donations are needed later
Body: Background: Donate Cash currently follows a platform-pooled funding model. Goal: Add in-site allocation tracking only if future scope requires it. Acceptance: Define allocation tables audit fields and display boundaries.

Title: OPS-01 Add retry and alerting for failed goods donation notifications
Body: Background: Email notification is already part of the main flow. Goal: Add retry logic and operational alerting. Acceptance: Failed sends are retryable and observable.

Title: FE-ARCH-01 Evaluate migrating the admin iframe host to native React modules
Body: Background: The admin preview still depends on an iframe host approach. Goal: Produce a migration assessment without forcing a rewrite in this round. Acceptance: Deliver an executable migration plan by layer.

Title: TEST-01 Create a real PostgreSQL and RLS local integration script
Body: Background: Some validation still depends on manual environment setup. Goal: Capture a repeatable local integration script for database and permission checks. Acceptance: The script can bring up the database permission validation and the main flow checks.

Title: QA-01 Run end-to-end validation with both Platform Admin and Local Food Bank Admin
Body: Background: Admin scope work is now in the implementation stage. Goal: Run the main flow with both Platform Admin and Local Food Bank Admin accounts. Acceptance: The visibility differences between the two roles are clear and verified.

Title: QA-02 Validate Home Donate Cash and Donate Goods page messaging
Body: Background: Public pages are being unified under a platform-level narrative. Goal: Review page copy and prompts line by line. Acceptance: Home Donate Cash and Donate Goods all use consistent wording.

Title: QA-03 Validate goods donation notification delivery and local admin visibility
Body: Background: Donate Goods has been reframed as a record plus notification plus coordination flow. Goal: Confirm emails are sent and local admins can see the record. Acceptance: Notification delivery is traceable and the local admin view includes the donation.

Title: QA-04 Validate cash donation visibility for platform admin and default hiding for local admin
Body: Background: Cash donations now follow the platform-pooled funding model. Goal: Verify the default visibility difference between platform and local admin. Acceptance: Platform Admin can see the record and Local Admin cannot see platform-level cash details by default.

Title: TEST-02 Add regression tests for applications admin by-code and redeem scope limits
Body: Background: Applications endpoints are starting to enforce food bank scope. Goal: Add regression coverage for by-code lookup and redeem actions. Acceptance: Local Admin cannot query or redeem across banks.

Title: BE-05 Restrict local admin exposure through inventory APIs
Body: Background: Inventory still follows a platform-oriented structure. Goal: Remove global inventory exposure for local admin accounts. Acceptance: Local Admin cannot access the platform-wide inventory view through APIs.

Title: FE-01 Unify Home page impact copy under a platform-wide narrative
Body: Background: The Home page still needs full alignment with the network and platform narrative. Goal: Adjust impact headings subcopy and metric explanations. Acceptance: The page no longer implies data from a single food bank only.

Title: FE-02 Update Donate Cash page and success message for platform-pooled funding
Body: Background: Donate Cash should not imply in-site fund allocation. Goal: Tighten form copy and the success message. Acceptance: The page clearly states platform-level pooled use and does not require a new allocation page.

Title: FE-03 Reframe Donate Goods as send to the selected food bank and follow up by their team
Body: Background: Donate Goods needs to be explicit about coordination and notification. Goal: Align page copy step descriptions and the success message. Acceptance: Users clearly understand the request is sent to the selected food bank.

Title: FS-01 Complete food_bank_id and food_bank_name across frontend user API and auth store
Body: Background: Admin scope needs complete frontend awareness of food bank ownership. Goal: Align types API DTOs and login state fields. Acceptance: Pages can consume user.food_bank_id and user.food_bank_name directly.

Title: BE-01 Add migrations for users.food_bank_id donations_cash.food_bank_id and food_banks.notification_email
Body: Background: The current business model needs database fields to carry scope and notification data. Goal: Complete the minimum migration for admin scope and notification email. Acceptance: The fields are in the database with indexes and foreign keys.

Title: BE-02 Apply admin food bank scope filtering across auth stats donations and applications
Body: Background: The unified admin UI depends on data scope rather than separate pages. Goal: Return scoped data across auth stats donations and applications. Acceptance: Platform Admin sees full data and Local Admin sees only local data.

Title: DB-01 Complete demo bootstrap data for Platform Admin Local Admin and notification_email
Body: Background: Demo and integration flows need stable master data. Goal: Fill bootstrap data for admin ownership and notification email. Acceptance: Platform admin local admin and food bank notification emails are demo-ready.

Title: BE-03 Send goods donation notifications to the selected food bank or fall back to operations email
Body: Background: Goods donation submissions must enter a real notification flow. Goal: Send to the food bank notification email first and use operations as fallback. Acceptance: Missing local email never causes record loss and the recipient is traceable.

Title: FE-04 Review admin dashboard scope labels and local admin module trimming
Body: Background: AdminDataDashboardPreview already consumes scope metadata. Goal: Confirm banner wording hidden modules and empty states. Acceptance: Local Admin cannot see platform-level cash panels or cross-bank analysis.

Title: FE-05 Review admin food management table visibility and role banner behavior
Body: Background: Food Management is the highest-risk page for local admin leakage. Goal: Recheck table visibility and role banner behavior. Acceptance: Local admins only see local goods applications packages and related tables.

Title: BE-04 Review auth token and /auth/me responses for food bank scope fields
Body: Background: Scope fields must exist in both token payloads and user profile APIs. Goal: Recheck login refresh and /auth/me field completeness. Acceptance: The frontend can reliably read food_bank_id and food_bank_name after login.

Title: TEST-03 Run regression coverage for admin scope across auth donation and application flows
Body: Background: This round changes auth donations and applications together. Goal: Confirm the new test coverage protects scope limits. Acceptance: Key pytest cases cover login donation and application scope scenarios.

Title: QA-05 Recheck consistency between Donate Goods live impact and dashboard metrics
Body: Background: Public impact and dashboard metrics previously risked drifting apart. Goal: Recheck the shared metrics across public and admin entry points. Acceptance: Key numbers and labels are aligned in both places.

Title: AUTH-01 Complete login registration refresh token and demo account foundations
Body: Background: The project already has a basic identity layer. Goal: Support the public supermarket and admin roles with core login flows. Acceptance: Login registration refresh token and demo accounts are working.

Title: FLOW-01 Complete the Find Food Bank to Application Form main flow
Body: Background: The public-side main journey had to work first. Goal: Support searching for a food bank and entering the application flow. Acceptance: Users can search choose a site and submit the application form.

Title: DON-BASE-01 Complete Donate Cash submission and persistence
Body: Background: Cash donation is one of the foundational public flows. Goal: Support form submission and backend persistence. Acceptance: Donate Cash can be submitted and returns a payment reference.

Title: DON-BASE-02 Complete Donate Goods submission with food bank selection flow
Body: Background: Goods donation is the basis of local ownership in this project. Goal: Support food bank selection and goods donation submission. Acceptance: Records store food bank metadata and donation items.

Title: ADMIN-01 Complete dashboard analytics APIs and preview host integration
Body: Background: The admin dashboard is a core page for platform operations visibility. Goal: Wire analytics APIs into the frontend preview host. Acceptance: The dashboard can already read live analytics data.

Title: ADMIN-02 Complete baseline Food Management modules for packages inventory low stock and restock
Body: Background: The admin operations side needs unified management for packages inventory and restock. Goal: Deliver the baseline modules and data tables first. Acceptance: Packages inventory low stock and restock are visible and operable in admin.

Title: OPS-BASE-01 Complete quick start demo seed and analytics data foundations
Body: Background: Course presentation depends on reliable local startup and seed data. Goal: Capture quick start demo bootstrap and analytics data support. Acceptance: The project can start quickly and has demo-ready data.

Title: ISSUE-S1-01 Frontend and backend DTOs did not fully match
Body: Background: During the first integration round multiple endpoints had field drift. Handling: Gradually align shared types and schemas. Current status: The baseline issue was identified and moved into later adapter cleanup.

Title: ISSUE-S1-02 Local startup ports and database credential expectations were inconsistent
Body: Background: Scripts environment variables and database setup steps were previously scattered. Handling: Add quick start conventions and database setup docs. Current status: The main issue has been documented and mitigated.

Title: ISSUE-S1-03 Some APIs returned 500 when the database was unavailable
Body: Background: Error semantics were inconsistent under environment failures. Handling: Introduce a unified 503 behavior and related tests. Current status: The core issue has been fixed.

Title: ISSUE-S2-01 Public impact and dashboard metrics risked drifting apart
Body: Background: Public pages and admin pages previously used different metric logic. Handling: Move to shared metrics and tighter page wording. Current status: Final review is in progress.

Title: ISSUE-S2-02 The dashboard reference HTML depended too heavily on mock data
Body: Background: The admin preview host originally relied on static data in the reference HTML. Handling: Replace it with live analytics injection step by step. Current status: The main path is connected and still under review.

Title: ISSUE-S2-03 Inventory serialization and restock value ranges were unstable
Body: Background: Fields such as inventory updated_at and urgency caused compatibility issues. Handling: Tighten schemas and API contracts. Current status: The main issue has been handled.

Title: ISSUE-S3-01 Local Admin still risks seeing platform-level cash or cross-bank global data
Body: Background: Reusing one admin UI makes data leakage the biggest risk. Handling: Tighten the flow through frontend trimming and backend filtering together. Current status: Sprint 3 work is in progress.

Title: ISSUE-S3-02 Scope fields were not fully wired through frontend backend and token payloads
Body: Background: Without complete scope fields the app cannot reliably separate platform and local admin roles. Handling: Add food_bank_id food_bank_name and token payload support. Current status: Sprint 3 work is in progress.

Title: ISSUE-S3-03 Goods donation notification email and food bank ownership must land together
Body: Background: Without notification email and bank ownership the record plus notification flow cannot complete. Handling: Add migration bootstrap support and email fallback behavior. Current status: Sprint 3 work is in progress.

Title: ISSUE-S3-04 Inventory still follows a platform-oriented structure and should not be forced into multi-bank inventory in this round
Body: Background: Full multi-bank inventory would expand the current scope too much. Handling: Limit this round to page and permission tightening and leave independent local inventory for phase two. Current status: Recorded as a structural limitation.
